creator: gokuvsvegita@gmail.com
text:
After using FreeBSD for a while I stumbled upon one weird bug(?):
Issuing '$mkdir foo/bar/now/here -p' or '$mkdir foo -v' creates a folder named '-p' or '-v' respectively.
It is in my habbit of passing -p and -v afterwards, so I can change it... but mkdir should never create a folder name '-p' or '-v', over which I can't even cd or rm.
